# CANON REFERENCE DOCUMENT
## The Crimson Covenant Chronicles

---

## 1. CHARACTER FACTS

### 1.1 Vera Drowned (Salt-Hand)

| Attribute | Fact |
|-----------|------|
| **Full Name** | Vera Drowned |
| **Title/Nickname** | Salt-Hand |
| **Actual Age** | 37 years |
| **Apparent Age** | Late 40s (appears 15 years older due to blood magic deterioration) |
| **Birth Year** | Year 258 (present story is Year 295) |
| **Birthplace** | Village in what is now the Saltwaste region |

**Physical Description:**
- Hair: Silver/gray (premature graying from blood magic)
- Eyes: Pale gray (like seawater over sand), lost original color
- Build: Wiry and compact, built for efficiency
- Left palm: Distinctive scar across palm (ritual cut from first Bloodwright working at age 13)
- Right hand: Calloused from blade work
- Tattoo: Crimson blood-drop behind left ear (marks Blooded status)
- Feet: Bare (discomfort with shoes; grew up without them)
- Blood magic activation: Veins become visible beneath skin as dark network spreading from contact point

**Family:**
- Mother: Drowned trying to save Vera during flooding when Vera was 7 years old (Year 265)
- Father: Unknown fate
- Daughter: Mira (see below)

**Abilities:**
- Vessel-class bloodforge potential (discovered age 13)
- Best close-combat specialist in Covenant history
- 37 confirmed kills during 15-year career
- Trained in blade work by Kael Drowned-Voss

**Career Timeline:**
- Age 7: Mother drowned (Year 265)
- Age 13: Recruited by Covenant after Vessel-class potential discovered (Year 271)
- Age 28: Retired after birth of daughter (Year 286)
- Age 37: Present story (Year 295)

**Key Relationships:**
- Blood-oath sibling: Kael Drowned-Voss
- Daughter: Mira (taken by Covenant in Chapter 5)

**Psychological Profile:**
- Core wound: Mother drowned trying to save her
- Lie she believes: "If I stay far enough away, the people I love will be safe from what I am"
- Speaks in fragments; comfortable with silence
- Uses Covenant death-reference phrases: "when I'm dead," "before you drown," "after I'm released"
- Rarely swears by Drowned God
- Voice is flat but controlled
- Rarely asks questions; when she does, they are precise (who, where, when)

---

### 1.2 Mira Drowned

| Attribute | Fact |
|-----------|------|
| **Full Name** | Mira (surname inherited from Vera) |
| **Age at Retirement** | Infant (Vera retired upon birth) |
| **Age at Kidnapping** | 5 years old |
| **Birth Year** | Year 290 |

**Physical Description:**
- Demonstrates spontaneous blood manipulation at age 4
- Vessel-class ability manifested naturally (1 in 10,000 bloodline carriers can use magic without training)

**Key Possessions:**
- Small polished shell gifted by Sister Brine at Tidewell Monastery (left deliberately on pillow during kidnapping; indicates forethought)
- Keepsake from Vera's past life (unspecified)

**Capabilities:**
- Can feel her own blood moving when submerged
- Chest does not burn during breath-holding like other children (indicates physical adaptation)
- Untrained Vessel-class potential (dangerous; can accidentally harm herself or others)

---

### 1.3 Kael Drowned-Voss

| Attribute | Fact |
|-----------|------|
| **Full Name** | Kael Drowned-Voss |
| **Relationship to Vera** | Blood-oath sibling; possibly romantic history |
| **Present Occupation** | Ferry operator in Lower Arches |
| **Physical Feature** | Scar on chin |

**Family:**
- Sister (name unspecified)
- Sister's children (hostages used by Covenant)

**History:**
- Taught Vera blade work
- Vouched for Vera when she wanted to leave the Covenant
- Betrayed Vera's location to Covenant four years ago (coerced, not voluntary)
- Has been using the Undercroft safehouse that he and Vera previously shared
- Wrote unsent letter to Vera explaining Covenant's coercion
- Letter hidden in his salt-vault

**Capabilities:**
- Can be tracked by Vera through blood-oath bond
- Has access to intelligence Vera lacks

---

### 1.4 Vex Morreth

| Attribute | Fact |
|-----------|------|
| **Title** | Primarch (current leader of Crimson Covenant) |
| **Background** | Former dockworker |
| **Former Rank** | Junior Bloodlord at time of Pale Tower Incident |

**History:**
- Killed previous Primarch Cressa Voidhand in single combat (Year 289)
- Took control establishing current leadership structure
- Considered most ruthless killer in Covenant history

**Current Actions:**
- Consolidating all Covenant cells into single command structure
- Removing rivals through Vera's contracts (including Bloodlord Saltwell)
- Controls the Pale Tower

---

### 1.5 Elder Salthorn

| Attribute | Fact |
|-----------|------|
| **Title** | Keeper of the Well |
| **Location** | Tidewell Monastery |
| **Stance** | Strict neutrality |
| **Family** | Niece: Sister Brine |

**Authority:**
- Can excommunicate anyone from temple services (death sentence in the Drowned Reaches)

---

### 1.6 Sister Brine

| Attribute | Fact |
|-----------|------|
| **Role** | Young priestess at Tidewell Monastery |
| **Family Connection** | Niece of Elder Salthorn |
| **Political Lean** | Increasing sympathy for Flotilla refugees |

**Actions:**
- Took Mira to healing gardens when Vera brought her for fever treatment
- Gave Mira a polished shell gift
- Said: "For the child who loves the water"

---

### 1.7 Supporting Characters

| Name | Role/Fact |
|------|-----------|
| **Cressa Voidhand** | Previous Primarch; attempted mass Council assassination (Year 289); killed by Vex Morreth |
| **Bloodlord Saltwell** | Old enemy of Vera; oversees Vessel transfers; runs secondary facility; ordered killed by Covenant in consolidation; Vera kills him at Pale Tower secondary entrance |
| **Harlan Moss** | Cedarhold timber merchant; selling tideglass-location intelligence to Dockward Council; Vera's first contract target; killed by Vera |
| **Aldric Voss** | Dockward Council member investigating Covenant; Vera's second contract target; killed by Vera |
| **Vellum** | Blood-broker in Gutter Market; survived six Primarchs; facilitated Vera's contracts |
| **Salt** | Apothecary in Undercroft; Vera saved her daughter in Saltwaste; recognizes Vera; provides herbs and information |
| **Morreth the First** | Founder of Crimson Covenant (Year 47); dockworker who discovered blood manipulation |
| **Fen** | Boy at Tidewell Monastery; challenged Mira to breath-holding contest |

---

## 2. POLITICAL FACTS

### 2.1 The Tripartite Power Structure

**Three Sources of Power in the Drowned Reaches:**

| Power | Domain | Source of Authority |
|-------|--------|---------------------|
| **Dockward Council** | Surface affairs, taxation, public works | Oligarchic election by property owners |
| **Crimson Covenant** | Underground economy (smuggling, assassination, blood magic, tideglass) | Information and violence |
| **Tidewell Monastery** | Spiritual authority and medical care | Keeper's excommunication power |

---

### 2.2 Dockward Council

- Governs Verath's surface affairs
- Controls Harbor Authority (only legal port in region)
- Extracts fees from every ship entering or leaving
- Elected by property owners (inherently oligarchic)
- Attempted to nationalize tideglass extraction 40 years ago (failed; three Council members killed)
- Harbor Authority chief comptroller killed (throat opened, floating in harbor)

---

### 2.3 Crimson Covenant

**Official Status:** Illegal but tolerated tacitly because eliminating them would collapse black market funding half of Verath's legitimate economy

**Internal Hierarchy:**

```
PRIMARCH (absolute ruler)
    ↓ (selected by council)
BLOODLORDS (5 total, oversee major operations)
    ↓
BLOODED (completed Trial of Three Cuts)
    ↓
TITHED (indentured via dangerous labor)
    ↓
OATHBOUND (external contractors via blood-contract)
```

**Rank Determination:** Blood meritocracy based on demonstrated capability

**Blood-oath Requirement:** Every member offers blood-oath to the Primarch

**Current Bloodlords:**
1. Verath cell
2. Cedarhold cell
3. Flotilla network
4. Poison trade
5. Tideglass harvesting operations

**The Trial of Three Cuts:**
- Test for Blooded membership
- Requirements: Kill three people in three days using three different methods
- Proves capability and commitment

---

### 2.4 Tidewell Monastery

**Authority:**
- Primary temple of Drowned God
- Only trained physicians in region
- Keeper can excommunicate (effectively death sentence)
- Maintains neutrality; young priestesses show increasing Flotilla sympathy

**The Truce of the Well:**
- Neutral ground rules
- Healers treat anyone who arrives without questions
- Even Covenant members observe the truce

---

### 2.5 Treaties and Alliances

**Treaty of the Saltline (Year 118):**
- Ended the Indenture Wars
- Established current political boundaries
- Covenant supported Verath defenders in exchange for official tolerance
- Cemented Covenant's position

---

## 3. TIMELINE

### 3.1 Historical Events

| Year | Event |
|------|-------|
| **Year 0** | **The Sinking** — Volcanic activity and tectonic settling flood lowlands over two decades; region transforms from fertile farmland to coastal archipelagos |
| **Year 47** | **Covenant's Founding** — Morreth the First discovers blood manipulation through ritual self-sacrifice; gathers refugees and criminals; within 20 years controls Verath's black market |
| **Year 112-118** | **Indenture Wars** — Cedarhold attempts to reconquer flooded territories; six-year conflict |
| **Year 118** | **Treaty of Saltline** — Cedarhold defeated; establishes current political boundaries; Covenant gains official tolerance |
| **Year 265** | Vera's mother drowns trying to save her during flooding |
| **
